narrative_para1: A junior Software Developer at an Indian tech company or product startup spends their week writing and reviewing code in Python, Java, or JavaScript, fixing bugs raised on Jira, and attending daily standups in an Agile sprint. Daily tasks include building REST APIs, writing unit tests, peer reviewing pull requests on GitHub, and deploying code through Jenkins or GitHub Actions CI/CD pipelines. They work within version control workflows, follow coding standards, and coordinate with QA engineers before releases. In Indian service companies, they may work on client-facing modules requiring VAPT compliance; in product startups, they work directly on the core product codebase with faster iteration cycles.
narrative_para2: With 55% AI exposure, this role is actively AI-Evolving. GitHub Copilot and Tabnine assist with code completion, boilerplate generation, and unit test scaffolding, covering a significant portion of routine coding tasks. ChatGPT and Claude are used for debugging assistance, code explanation, and generating first-draft implementations. AI accelerates repetitive development tasks but requires human review for correctness, security, and architectural fit. However, software developers make architectural decisions, define abstractions, and design systems that meet complex, evolving business requirements. They debug intricate production issues involving concurrency, distributed systems, or data corruption where AI lacks sufficient context. Code review, mentoring junior developers, and aligning technical solutions to product goals demand human communication and accountability. The tools currently reshaping this role include GitHub Copilot, ChatGPT, and Tabnine — each accelerating routine work while leaving the most complex, context-dependent decisions firmly in human hands.
narrative_para3: B.Tech, B.Sc (CS), MCA, or M.Tech graduates are the core fit. Those with strong problem-solving instincts, comfort with reading documentation, and the ability to break complex requirements into working code will thrive. Starting salaries of Rs.3.5-6 lakh grow to Rs.7-12 lakh at 2-3 years, Rs.12-20 lakh at senior developer level, and Rs.20-40 lakh for tech leads and architects. DSA (Data Structures and Algorithms) proficiency is the most critical hiring filter. Open source contributions and strong GitHub profiles accelerate progression. Eligible programme levels: UG, PG.

narrative_para1: A junior Full Stack Developer at an Indian product startup or digital agency spends their week building both frontend React.js components and backend Node.js or Python APIs, connecting them to PostgreSQL or MongoDB databases. Daily tasks include designing and consuming REST APIs, debugging issues that span frontend and server layers, writing database queries, and deploying features through GitHub Actions or Vercel. They participate in sprint planning, estimate development effort, and coordinate with UI/UX designers to implement pixel-accurate interfaces. In Indian SaaS companies, they often work across the full feature stack — from database schema changes to the final rendered UI — with minimal handoffs.
narrative_para2: With 60% AI exposure, this role is actively AI-Evolving. v0 by Vercel generates full React component scaffolding from natural language, dramatically accelerating frontend development. GitHub Copilot assists across both frontend and backend codebases, completing boilerplate and suggesting API integrations. ChatGPT supports architecture decisions, SQL query generation, and debugging complex stack traces. However, full stack developers integrate disparate systems, balance performance trade-offs across frontend and backend, and make product-aligned architectural choices that AI cannot assess without deep business context. They own the end-to-end delivery of features including deployment, monitoring, and incident response. Debugging cross-layer issues — where a frontend behaviour is caused by a backend race condition — requires holistic system thinking. The tools currently reshaping this role include GitHub Copilot, v0 by Vercel, and ChatGPT — each accelerating routine work while leaving the most complex, context-dependent decisions firmly in human hands.
narrative_para3: B.Tech, BCA, or MCA graduates with hands-on project experience across both frontend and backend are the core fit. Those who enjoy end-to-end product ownership and are comfortable context-switching between UI logic and server-side concerns will excel. Starting at Rs.4-7 lakh, salaries grow to Rs.8-14 lakh at 2-3 years, Rs.14-22 lakh at senior level, and Rs.22-45 lakh for tech leads. A strong portfolio of deployed projects — visible on GitHub or live URLs — is the most effective differentiator. Eligible programme levels: UG, PG.

narrative_para1: A junior Frontend Developer at an Indian tech company or digital agency spends their week building responsive user interfaces in React.js and TypeScript, implementing designs handed off from Figma, and ensuring cross-browser compatibility across Chrome, Firefox, and Safari. Daily tasks include creating reusable UI components with Tailwind CSS, optimising page load times, fixing CSS layout bugs, and integrating frontend views with backend APIs. They work within component libraries and design systems, collaborate closely with UI/UX designers to match specifications, and write unit tests using Jest. Indian product companies increasingly expect accessibility (WCAG) compliance and Core Web Vitals awareness even at junior levels.
narrative_para2: With 65% AI exposure, this role is actively AI-Evolving. v0 by Vercel and Lovable generate complete React component trees, forms, and UI layouts from text descriptions, covering a large share of routine frontend work. GitHub Copilot completes CSS, component logic, and test cases in real time. AI tools are transforming frontend development speed but human review remains essential for accessibility, performance, and brand consistency. However, frontend developers translate design system requirements into pixel-perfect, accessible, and performant interfaces, requiring aesthetic judgement and user empathy. They debug browser-specific rendering issues and performance bottlenecks requiring deep knowledge of DOM, rendering engines, and paint cycles. Collaboration with designers and product managers to refine UX details demands communication skills beyond AI capability. The tools currently reshaping this role include GitHub Copilot, v0 by Vercel, and Lovable (AI App Builder) — each accelerating routine work while leaving the most complex, context-dependent decisions firmly in human hands.
narrative_para3: B.Tech, BCA, or BDes graduates with a strong portfolio of web projects are well suited. Those with a sharp eye for visual detail, patience with browser-specific quirks, and genuine interest in how users experience interfaces will excel. Starting at Rs.3.5-6 lakh, salaries grow to Rs.7-12 lakh at 2-3 years, Rs.12-20 lakh at senior level, and Rs.20-38 lakh for frontend architects or UI engineering leads. A live portfolio demonstrating responsive, accessible, and well-structured projects is the single strongest differentiator at hiring. Eligible programme levels: UG, PG.

narrative_para1: A junior Backend Developer at an Indian product or fintech company spends their week writing server-side APIs in Java (Spring Boot) or Python (FastAPI/Django), designing database schemas in PostgreSQL, and integrating third-party services like payment gateways (Razorpay, PayU) or SMS providers. Daily tasks include writing business logic, building RESTful APIs consumed by frontend or mobile clients, writing SQL queries, and handling authentication flows using JWT or OAuth. They work within microservices or monolithic architectures depending on company maturity, deploy via Docker containers, and monitor API performance using Datadog or New Relic. In Indian fintech contexts, they handle RBI-mandated encryption standards and data handling requirements for sensitive financial data.
narrative_para2: With 55% AI exposure, this role is actively AI-Evolving. GitHub Copilot and Tabnine accelerate boilerplate generation for service layers, repository patterns, and unit tests in Java and Python backends. ChatGPT assists in designing database schemas, writing complex SQL queries, and generating API documentation. AI code generation requires careful human review for security vulnerabilities, especially in authentication, authorisation, and data handling code. However, backend developers design fault-tolerant distributed systems, select appropriate data stores, and make latency vs consistency trade-offs requiring deep systems thinking. They debug race conditions, deadlocks, and data inconsistencies in production requiring understanding of system internals AI cannot replicate. Security-critical code paths, performance optimisation under load, and disaster recovery design require human expertise and accountability. The tools currently reshaping this role include GitHub Copilot, ChatGPT, and Tabnine — each accelerating routine work while leaving the most complex, context-dependent decisions firmly in human hands.
narrative_para3: B.Tech or MCA graduates with solid DSA fundamentals and server-side programming experience are the core fit. Those who enjoy designing systems, thinking about failure modes, and solving performance problems at scale will thrive. Starting at Rs.4-7 lakh, salaries grow to Rs.8-14 lakh at 2-3 years, Rs.14-22 lakh at senior level, and Rs.22-45 lakh for principal engineers. Leetcode proficiency and system design knowledge are the primary hiring filters at product companies. Eligible programme levels: UG, PG.

narrative_para1: A junior Mobile App Developer at an Indian startup or digital product company spends their week building features in Flutter or Kotlin for Android, and occasionally Swift for iOS, writing UI components, integrating REST APIs, and managing local state using Provider or Riverpod. Daily tasks include handling device permissions, integrating Firebase for push notifications and analytics, testing on multiple Android screen sizes and API levels, and submitting builds through the Google Play Console. Indian app developers frequently account for low-bandwidth environments, feature phone form factors, and regional language support. They coordinate with backend developers on API contracts and with QA engineers to reproduce device-specific bugs before release.
narrative_para2: With 55% AI exposure, this role is actively AI-Evolving. GitHub Copilot accelerates Flutter widget trees, Kotlin coroutines boilerplate, and SwiftUI components. ChatGPT supports integration of third-party SDKs, resolving platform-specific build errors, and generating localisation strings. AI tools are widely used for generating unit tests for ViewModels and business logic layers in mobile codebases. However, mobile app developers optimise for platform-specific behaviours, device fragmentation, and battery and memory constraints that AI cannot contextualise fully. They navigate App Store and Play Store review guidelines, handle push notification flows, and implement biometric authentication with platform-specific nuances. Designing offline-first architectures and handling poor network connectivity in Indian market contexts requires domain experience. The tools currently reshaping this role include GitHub Copilot, ChatGPT, and Tabnine — each accelerating routine work while leaving the most complex, context-dependent decisions firmly in human hands.
narrative_para3: B.Tech, BCA, or MCA graduates with hands-on Flutter or Kotlin experience are well positioned. Those who enjoy building polished end-user experiences, care about app performance and battery life, and are patient with platform-specific idiosyncrasies will thrive. Starting at Rs.3.5-6 lakh, salaries grow to Rs.7-13 lakh at 2-3 years, Rs.13-22 lakh at senior developer level, and Rs.22-40 lakh for mobile tech leads. Published apps on Play Store or App Store are a strong portfolio signal. Eligible programme levels: UG, PG.

narrative_para1: A junior QA Engineer at an Indian software company spends their week writing and executing manual test cases, creating automated test scripts in Selenium or Cypress, and logging bugs in Jira with detailed reproduction steps. Daily tasks include testing API endpoints with Postman, performing regression testing before releases, participating in sprint demos, and coordinating with developers on bug fixes. They maintain test plans aligned to user stories, run smoke and sanity tests on staging environments, and support UAT by preparing test data. Indian service companies often require QA engineers to work across functional, regression, performance (JMeter), and API testing tracks simultaneously, making breadth of testing knowledge essential.
narrative_para2: With 55% AI exposure, this role is actively AI-Evolving. GitHub Copilot and ChatGPT generate Selenium and Cypress test scripts from user story descriptions, significantly reducing test authoring time. AI tools assist in generating edge case test data, creating API mock configurations, and writing BDD step definitions. AI-powered test generation is emerging for visual regression testing and exploratory test suggestions. However, qA engineers apply domain knowledge and user empathy to identify edge cases and real-world usage scenarios that automated generation misses. They assess risk-based test prioritisation, balancing coverage with release velocity in ways that require business context. Root cause analysis of flaky tests, environment-specific failures, and third-party integration issues requires investigative skills AI cannot replicate. The tools currently reshaping this role include GitHub Copilot, ChatGPT, and Tabnine — each accelerating routine work while leaving the most complex, context-dependent decisions firmly in human hands.
narrative_para3: B.Tech, BCA, or MCA graduates are the core fit. Those with systematic thinking, curiosity to break things, and patience with repetitive testing cycles will find the role fulfilling. Starting at Rs.3-5 lakh, salaries grow to Rs.6-10 lakh at 2-3 years, Rs.10-17 lakh at senior QA level, and Rs.17-30 lakh for QA leads and test architects. ISTQB certification is a widely recognised qualifier. Shifting into automation-heavy roles with strong Selenium and Python skills accelerates salary growth significantly. Eligible programme levels: UG, PG.

narrative_para1: A junior DevOps Engineer at an Indian tech company spends their week writing Terraform scripts to provision AWS infrastructure, building and maintaining CI/CD pipelines in Jenkins or GitHub Actions, and managing containerised workloads on Kubernetes. Daily tasks include monitoring application health through Datadog or Grafana dashboards, triaging alerts, optimising Docker image builds, and supporting development teams with deployment issues. They maintain cloud infrastructure as code, manage secret rotation in AWS Secrets Manager or HashiCorp Vault, and participate in on-call rotations. Indian product companies expect DevOps engineers to implement cost-optimised cloud architectures given the sensitivity to AWS/Azure bills in growth-stage startups.
narrative_para2: With 55% AI exposure, this role is actively AI-Evolving. GitHub Copilot and ChatGPT accelerate writing Terraform modules, Kubernetes manifests, and Dockerfile configurations. Amazon Q Developer assists with AWS-specific IaC and CloudFormation templates. AI tools assist in root cause analysis during incidents by correlating logs and suggesting runbook steps, though final remediation requires human judgement. However, devOps engineers design resilient deployment architectures that balance speed, safety, and cost for specific organisational contexts. They conduct blameless post-mortems and drive systemic improvements to prevent recurring failures, requiring organisational trust and communication skills. Production incident response — assessing blast radius, coordinating rollbacks, and communicating to stakeholders — demands human accountability. The tools currently reshaping this role include GitHub Copilot, ChatGPT, and Amazon Q Developer (formerly AWS CodeWhisperer) — each accelerating routine work while leaving the most complex, context-dependent decisions firmly in human hands.
narrative_para3: B.Tech or MCA graduates with hands-on Linux, cloud, and scripting experience are well suited. Those who enjoy the intersection of infrastructure, automation, and reliability — and can stay calm during production incidents — will thrive. Starting at Rs.4.5-7 lakh, salaries grow to Rs.9-15 lakh at 2-3 years, Rs.15-25 lakh at senior level, and Rs.25-50 lakh for DevOps architects and SRE leads. AWS/Azure certifications (Solutions Architect Associate, DevOps Professional) are widely valued and accelerate promotion. Eligible programme levels: UG, PG.

narrative_para1: A junior Cloud Engineer at an Indian tech company or IT services firm spends their week provisioning and managing cloud infrastructure on AWS, Azure, or GCP using Terraform or CloudFormation, configuring VPCs, security groups, IAM roles, and S3 buckets following least-privilege principles. Daily tasks include monitoring cloud costs and setting budget alerts, supporting application teams with cloud resource requests, managing container workloads on EKS or AKS, and responding to infrastructure incidents. They implement automated patching and compliance checks, support RBI or SEBI data residency requirements for BFSI clients, and assist in cloud migration assessments for legacy on-premise systems being moved to cloud.
narrative_para2: With 55% AI exposure, this role is actively AI-Evolving. Amazon Q Developer assists with AWS-specific IaC generation, security configuration auditing, and architecture optimisation recommendations. GitHub Copilot accelerates Terraform module writing, cloud-init scripts, and policy document generation. AI tools increasingly help identify cost anomalies and suggest rightsizing recommendations in cloud environments. However, cloud engineers design multi-region architectures that balance resilience, latency, compliance, and cost — decisions requiring business context and risk judgement. They architect data residency solutions for Indian regulatory requirements such as RBI and SEBI data localisation mandates and advise on cloud migration strategies. Security architecture decisions affecting customer data require human accountability. The tools currently reshaping this role include Amazon Q Developer (formerly AWS CodeWhisperer), GitHub Copilot, and ChatGPT — each accelerating routine work while leaving the most complex, context-dependent decisions firmly in human hands.
narrative_para3: B.Tech or MCA graduates with foundational Linux and networking knowledge are well positioned. Those who enjoy infrastructure problem-solving, cost optimisation puzzles, and are comfortable with multi-service cloud ecosystems will thrive. Starting at Rs.4.5-8 lakh, salaries grow to Rs.10-16 lakh at 2-3 years, Rs.16-26 lakh at senior cloud engineer level, and Rs.26-55 lakh for cloud architects. AWS Certified Solutions Architect and Azure Administrator certifications are the most effective salary accelerators in this field. Eligible programme levels: UG, PG.

narrative_para1: A junior Data Analyst at an Indian e-commerce, fintech, or consumer internet company spends their week writing SQL queries to pull data from warehouse platforms like Snowflake or Redshift, building Power BI or Tableau dashboards for business teams, and preparing weekly performance reports for product and marketing stakeholders. Daily tasks include cleaning raw data in Python (Pandas), conducting cohort analyses, tracking KPIs like DAU, retention rates, and conversion funnels, and responding to ad hoc data requests from business leads. They coordinate with data engineers on pipeline issues, validate data quality, and present findings in weekly business review meetings — often to non-technical audiences who need clear, jargon-free narratives.
narrative_para2: With 60% AI exposure, this role is actively AI-Evolving. ChatGPT and Power BI Copilot automate SQL query generation, data cleaning scripts, and dashboard commentary, covering a large share of routine analyst output. Copilot in Excel accelerates pivot table analysis, formula writing, and data exploration. AI tools assist in anomaly detection and generating first-draft analytical reports. However, data analysts frame the right business questions, contextualise data anomalies against operational events, and translate insights into actionable recommendations that AI cannot generate without business context. They build relationships with business stakeholders to understand decision needs and communicate findings persuasively to non-technical audiences. Ethical handling of sensitive customer data and bias identification in analyses requires human judgement. The tools currently reshaping this role include ChatGPT, Power BI Copilot, and Copilot in Excel (Microsoft 365 Copilot) — each accelerating routine work while leaving the most complex, context-dependent decisions firmly in human hands.
narrative_para3: B.Tech, BCA, B.Sc (Statistics/Mathematics), MBA, or M.Sc graduates are well suited. Those who enjoy finding stories in numbers, are curious about business outcomes, and can communicate clearly with non-technical stakeholders will thrive. Starting at Rs.3-5 lakh, salaries grow to Rs.6-11 lakh at 2-3 years, Rs.10-18 lakh at senior analyst level, and Rs.18-35 lakh for analytics managers. SQL and Python proficiency combined with strong data visualisation skills (Power BI or Tableau) are the core hiring requirements. Eligible programme levels: UG, PG, MBA.

narrative_para1: A junior Data Engineer at an Indian tech or fintech company spends their week building and maintaining ETL and ELT pipelines using Apache Airflow, dbt, or AWS Glue, ingesting data from transactional databases and third-party APIs into Snowflake or Redshift data warehouses. Daily tasks include writing PySpark jobs for large-scale transformations, debugging pipeline failures caused by schema changes or upstream API breaks, monitoring data freshness dashboards, and improving data quality checks using Great Expectations or dbt tests. They document data lineage and work with data analysts to build reliable datasets that power business dashboards, ensuring data contracts between source systems and downstream consumers are maintained.
narrative_para2: With 55% AI exposure, this role is actively AI-Evolving. GitHub Copilot and ChatGPT generate PySpark jobs, dbt models, and Airflow DAG boilerplate, significantly reducing pipeline development time. Amazon Q Developer assists with AWS Glue and Redshift query optimisation. AI tools help with SQL transformation logic, schema documentation, and data lineage annotation. However, data engineers design data architectures that balance freshness, cost, scalability, and governance for specific organisational data maturity levels. They troubleshoot complex data quality failures, schema drift, and pipeline failures requiring deep knowledge of upstream source systems. Cross-functional coordination with data scientists, analysts, and platform teams to align on data contracts and SLAs requires human relationship management. The tools currently reshaping this role include GitHub Copilot, ChatGPT, and Amazon Q Developer (formerly AWS CodeWhisperer) — each accelerating routine work while leaving the most complex, context-dependent decisions firmly in human hands.
narrative_para3: B.Tech or MCA graduates with strong SQL, Python, and cloud fundamentals are the core fit. Those who enjoy building infrastructure that others rely on, care deeply about data reliability, and are comfortable debugging complex distributed pipeline failures will thrive. Starting at Rs.4.5-8 lakh, salaries grow to Rs.9-16 lakh at 2-3 years, Rs.16-26 lakh at senior data engineer level, and Rs.26-50 lakh for data platform leads. Cloud data certifications and hands-on dbt or Spark project experience are strong differentiators. Eligible programme levels: UG, PG.

narrative_para1: A junior Data Scientist at an Indian product company or analytics firm spends their week conducting exploratory data analysis in Jupyter notebooks using Python and Pandas, building and evaluating ML models with Scikit-learn or XGBoost, and validating model performance on held-out test sets. Daily tasks include feature engineering from raw transactional data, running A/B test analyses, preparing model performance reports for product stakeholders, and collaborating with data engineers to productionise models in MLflow. They work on problems like churn prediction, fraud detection, recommendation engines, or demand forecasting — use cases common in Indian e-commerce, fintech, and edtech companies. Communicating model trade-offs clearly to non-technical product managers is a recurring expectation.
narrative_para2: With 60% AI exposure, this role is actively AI-Evolving. ChatGPT and GitHub Copilot accelerate exploratory data analysis, Jupyter notebook code generation, and ML pipeline boilerplate. Perplexity AI assists with literature review for algorithms, model selection rationale, and benchmarking. AutoML tools and AI-assisted hyperparameter tuning reduce experimentation cycle time significantly. However, data scientists frame the right problem statement and translate fuzzy business questions into well-defined ML tasks, a step requiring domain knowledge AI cannot supply. They critically evaluate model fairness, interpretability, and business impact before recommending deployment, ensuring responsible AI use. Communicating model limitations, confidence intervals, and bias risks to non-technical stakeholders requires deep technical communication skills. The tools currently reshaping this role include ChatGPT, GitHub Copilot, and Perplexity AI — each accelerating routine work while leaving the most complex, context-dependent decisions firmly in human hands.
narrative_para3: M.Tech, M.Sc (Statistics/Mathematics/CS), or MBA graduates with strong quantitative foundations are well positioned. Those who enjoy combining statistical rigour with software engineering to solve real business problems will thrive. Starting at Rs.4.5-8 lakh, salaries grow to Rs.9-16 lakh at 2-3 years, Rs.16-28 lakh at senior data scientist level, and Rs.28-60 lakh for principal scientists and AI research leads. Strong Kaggle profiles, research publications, or demonstrable industry ML projects are significant hiring differentiators. Eligible programme level: PG.

narrative_para1: A junior ML Engineer at an Indian AI-first startup or large tech company spends their week writing PyTorch or TensorFlow training pipelines, building data preprocessing scripts, and setting up model evaluation frameworks in MLflow. Daily tasks include optimising model inference latency for production APIs, containerising models in Docker for deployment on Kubernetes, monitoring model performance metrics for drift, and debugging training failures caused by data pipeline bugs or GPU memory constraints. They work closely with data scientists to translate experimental notebooks into production-grade code, maintain feature stores, and implement A/B testing infrastructure for model comparisons. Indian AI companies increasingly build on top of open-source LLMs for NLP use cases, making Hugging Face ecosystem familiarity highly valued.
narrative_para2: With 65% AI exposure, this role is actively AI-Evolving. GitHub Copilot and ChatGPT generate training scripts, data loaders, and model evaluation code, covering a significant share of ML engineering boilerplate. AI tools assist in hyperparameter search scripting, Docker image optimisation for inference, and Kubernetes deployment manifest generation. The role itself involves building AI systems — making it both a producer and consumer of AI tooling. However, mL engineers design reliable, scalable model serving infrastructure that meets latency SLOs and handles traffic spikes — decisions requiring systems thinking beyond what AI tools can automate. They debug subtle model degradation issues in production, tracing root causes through data drift, pipeline bugs, or distribution shift. Setting up responsible monitoring frameworks and escalation protocols for model failures requires human judgement. The tools currently reshaping this role include GitHub Copilot, ChatGPT, and Perplexity AI — each accelerating routine work while leaving the most complex, context-dependent decisions firmly in human hands.
narrative_para3: M.Tech (CS/AI/ML), M.Sc (Statistics or Mathematics), or graduates with strong ML research or project backgrounds are the core fit. Those who enjoy the intersection of software engineering and machine learning — building systems that are reliable in production, not just accurate in notebooks — will thrive. Starting at Rs.5-9 lakh, salaries grow to Rs.10-18 lakh at 2-3 years, Rs.18-30 lakh at senior ML engineer level, and Rs.30-65 lakh (VERIFY) for staff ML engineers and AI leads. Publications, Kaggle master status, or open-source ML contributions are strong differentiators. Eligible programme level: PG.

narrative_para1: A junior Cybersecurity Analyst at an Indian IT services firm, bank, or enterprise security team spends their week monitoring SIEM dashboards for security alerts, investigating suspicious network traffic using Wireshark and the ELK Stack, and conducting vulnerability assessments using Nmap and Burp Suite. Daily tasks include triaging alerts from EDR and SIEM tools, reviewing firewall and proxy logs for anomalies, performing patch compliance checks, and supporting security awareness training initiatives. They assist in VAPT (Vulnerability Assessment and Penetration Testing) exercises, prepare reports for CERT-In compliance, and coordinate with the IT team on remediation of identified vulnerabilities. Indian BFSI and government sectors have active SIEM monitoring mandates, creating steady demand.
narrative_para2: With 40% AI exposure, this role is actively AI-Evolving. Microsoft Copilot and ChatGPT assist in threat intelligence research, generating security policy templates, and drafting incident response runbooks. AI-powered SIEM tools increasingly correlate security events and generate initial triage recommendations. Perplexity AI helps analysts research new CVEs, attack techniques, and threat actor TTPs rapidly. However, cybersecurity analysts apply contextual judgement to distinguish genuine attacks from false positives in noisy alert environments — a skill requiring experience AI cannot replicate. They conduct adversarial thinking, simulating attacker behaviour to identify novel attack vectors not covered by signature-based detection. Incident response coordination, regulatory reporting to CERT-In, and stakeholder communication during breaches require human accountability. The tools currently reshaping this role include Microsoft Copilot, ChatGPT, and Perplexity AI — each accelerating routine work while leaving the most complex, context-dependent decisions firmly in human hands.
narrative_para3: B.Tech or MCA graduates with networking fundamentals and security interest are well positioned. Those with an adversarial mindset, patience for log analysis, and genuine curiosity about how attacks work will thrive. Starting at Rs.4-7 lakh, salaries grow to Rs.8-14 lakh at 2-3 years, Rs.14-24 lakh at senior analyst level, and Rs.24-50 lakh for CISO-track roles. CEH, CompTIA Security+, and OSCP certifications are the most valued in the Indian cybersecurity market. Eligible programme levels: UG, PG.

narrative_para1: A junior Network Administrator at an Indian enterprise, IT services company, or data centre spends their week managing Cisco and Juniper switches and routers, configuring VLANs, and monitoring network uptime through tools like SolarWinds or PRTG. Daily tasks include managing DNS and DHCP configurations, troubleshooting connectivity issues reported by end users, updating firewall rules in line with change management processes, and maintaining network diagrams and asset registers. They support Wi-Fi access point management, VPN configurations for remote users, and ISP link failover setups. In Indian enterprises, network administrators often manage hybrid environments combining on-premise infrastructure with Azure or AWS connectivity through Direct Connect or ExpressRoute circuits.
narrative_para2: With 30% AI exposure, this role is actively AI-Evolving. ChatGPT and Microsoft Copilot assist in drafting network configuration scripts, generating firewall rule documentation, and researching CVEs for network equipment. AI-powered network monitoring tools automate anomaly detection and alert correlation, reducing manual log review. AI assists in generating network topology documentation and change management records. However, network administrators make real-time decisions during network outages that affect business continuity, requiring rapid problem-solving under pressure. They design network segmentation, firewall policies, and access control lists that balance security with business usability. Vendor negotiations, ISP escalations, and infrastructure change management require human relationship skills and accountability. The tools currently reshaping this role include ChatGPT, Microsoft Copilot, and Perplexity AI — each accelerating routine work while leaving the most complex, context-dependent decisions firmly in human hands.
narrative_para3: B.Tech, BCA, or Diploma in Networking graduates are well positioned. Those who are methodical, comfortable with hardware-level troubleshooting, and can stay calm during network outages will thrive. This is predominantly an on-site role given the physical infrastructure dependency. Starting at Rs.3-5 lakh, salaries grow to Rs.6-10 lakh at 2-3 years, Rs.10-16 lakh at senior level, and Rs.16-28 lakh for network architects. CCNA and CCNP certifications are the primary career progression credentials in this field. Eligible programme levels: UG, PG.

narrative_para1: A junior Database Administrator at an Indian enterprise or IT services company spends their week managing Oracle, PostgreSQL, or Microsoft SQL Server databases, monitoring query performance through execution plan analysis, and ensuring database backup schedules run successfully and recovery tests pass. Daily tasks include responding to slow query escalations from developers, reviewing and approving index creation requests, managing user access permissions, and applying security patches during planned maintenance windows. They maintain database capacity reports, support ETL teams with database connectivity issues, and coordinate with the cloud team on RDS instance sizing for AWS-hosted databases. Indian BFSI clients often require Oracle-certified DBAs for core banking system support.
narrative_para2: With 40% AI exposure, this role is actively AI-Evolving. ChatGPT and GitHub Copilot assist in generating complex SQL queries, stored procedures, and index recommendation scripts. AI tools are increasingly used to suggest query optimisation strategies by analysing execution plans. Automated database monitoring tools use AI to predict storage growth, detect slow queries, and recommend maintenance windows. However, dBAs make critical decisions on storage architecture, backup retention, and recovery objectives that balance cost with business risk — requiring contextual judgement about data criticality. They diagnose complex performance degradation caused by lock contention, statistics staleness, or storage I/O saturation, requiring deep platform expertise. Database migration planning during major upgrades requires careful testing and rollback planning that demands human accountability. The tools currently reshaping this role include ChatGPT, GitHub Copilot, and Copilot in Excel (Microsoft 365 Copilot) — each accelerating routine work while leaving the most complex, context-dependent decisions firmly in human hands.
narrative_para3: B.Tech or MCA graduates with strong SQL fundamentals and interest in data infrastructure are well suited. Those who are methodical, enjoy performance tuning puzzles, and can handle the on-call responsibility of database incidents will thrive. Starting at Rs.3.5-5.5 lakh, salaries grow to Rs.7-12 lakh at 2-3 years, Rs.12-20 lakh at senior DBA level, and Rs.20-35 lakh for database architects. Oracle DBA certification and PostgreSQL expertise are strong market differentiators in India. Eligible programme levels: UG, PG.

narrative_para1: A junior UI/UX Designer at an Indian product startup or digital agency spends their week creating wireframes and high-fidelity prototypes in Figma, conducting user interviews and usability tests, and iterating on designs based on product manager and developer feedback. Daily tasks include building responsive layouts, defining interaction states and microanimations, preparing developer handoff specifications using Figma Dev Mode, and maintaining the company's design system. They research competitor interfaces, prepare user journey maps, and present design decisions in sprint reviews. Indian product designers are expected to be fluent in designing for mobile-first experiences across both Android and iOS platforms, accounting for diverse Indian user contexts including vernacular language interfaces and low-data environments.
narrative_para2: With 60% AI exposure, this role is actively AI-Evolving. Midjourney and DALL-E generate visual concepts, mood boards, and illustration assets that replace hours of manual design work. v0 by Vercel generates interactive UI components directly from design descriptions, bridging design-to-code. AI tools assist in synthesising user research data, generating persona drafts, and producing accessibility audit reports. However, uI/UX designers conduct in-depth user interviews and contextual enquiries to uncover latent needs and pain points that AI cannot discover without human empathy. They make nuanced aesthetic decisions reflecting brand values, cultural context, and emotional resonance with Indian users. Design system governance, aligning designers and developers on component usage, and managing design critique sessions require human leadership. The tools currently reshaping this role include Midjourney, DALL-E, v0 by Vercel, and Canva AI — each accelerating routine work while leaving the most complex, context-dependent decisions firmly in human hands.
narrative_para3: B.Des, BFA, B.Tech, or HCI postgraduate graduates are well suited. Those with a blend of visual design sensibility, empathy for diverse users, and comfort with rapid iteration will thrive. Building a polished Figma portfolio demonstrating end-to-end design thinking — from user research to pixel-perfect UI — is the single strongest hiring differentiator. Starting at Rs.3.5-6 lakh, salaries grow to Rs.7-13 lakh at 2-3 years, Rs.13-22 lakh at senior designer level, and Rs.22-40 lakh for design leads. Eligible programme levels: UG, PG.

narrative_para1: A junior Associate Product Manager at an Indian product startup or tech company spends their week writing Product Requirement Documents (PRDs), translating business goals into user stories in Jira, and facilitating sprint planning with engineering and design teams. Daily tasks include analysing product metrics dashboards in Mixpanel or Amplitude, conducting user interviews, reviewing competitor app store updates, and resolving scope ambiguity during sprint execution. They prepare weekly product update decks for leadership, prioritise bug and feature backlogs using frameworks like RICE or MoSCoW, and track OKRs for their product area. Indian product roles increasingly expect comfort with SQL for self-serve data analysis, reducing dependence on data analyst teams for routine product queries.
narrative_para2: With 55% AI exposure, this role is actively AI-Evolving. ChatGPT and Notion AI accelerate PRD drafting, user story generation, competitor analysis, and meeting notes summarisation. Perplexity AI is used for rapid market research, benchmarking, and feature discovery across competitor products. AI tools assist in generating data analysis queries and interpreting product metrics dashboards. However, associate product managers build deep empathy with users through interviews, usability studies, and field observations that AI cannot replicate. They navigate complex stakeholder politics, aligning engineering, design, and business teams around a shared product vision — requiring trust, communication, and influence skills. Prioritisation decisions involving real trade-offs between user needs, business goals, and technical constraints require human judgement. The tools currently reshaping this role include ChatGPT, Notion AI, and Perplexity AI — each accelerating routine work while leaving the most complex, context-dependent decisions firmly in human hands.
narrative_para3: MBA, B.Tech, or any graduate with demonstrated product thinking and analytical ability are well positioned. Those with strong communication skills, comfort with ambiguity, and genuine curiosity about user behaviour will excel. The role rewards those who can earn trust from both engineering teams and business stakeholders simultaneously. Starting at Rs.4.5-8 lakh, salaries grow to Rs.10-18 lakh at 2-3 years, Rs.18-30 lakh at PM level, and Rs.30-60 lakh for senior PM and GPM roles at product-led companies. APM programmes at Indian product companies are highly competitive entry pathways. Eligible programme levels: UG, PG, MBA.

narrative_para1: A junior Technical Writer at an Indian SaaS company or IT services firm spends their week writing API reference documentation, developer getting-started guides, and end-user help articles, using tools like Confluence, Notion, or Readme.io. Daily tasks include testing product features first-hand to verify documentation accuracy, coordinating with developers and product managers to understand new feature specifications, editing and structuring existing documentation for clarity and findability, and maintaining changelog entries for product releases. They work closely with engineering teams to document REST API endpoints, SDKs, and integration workflows, often using Git for version-controlled docs-as-code workflows. Indian SaaS companies targeting global developer audiences require technical writers fluent in international English conventions and API documentation standards.
narrative_para2: With 70% AI exposure, this role is actively AI-Evolving. ChatGPT and Copilot in Word draft first versions of API documentation, user guides, and release notes from specifications or code, covering a large portion of routine writing. Grammarly automates grammar, clarity, and style consistency checking. Notion AI accelerates knowledge base structuring, doc summarisation, and internal wiki maintenance. However, technical writers ensure accuracy through hands-on product testing, reproducing developer workflows to identify undocumented edge cases and errors that AI-generated docs routinely miss. They develop documentation information architecture and taxonomy that reflects how users actually think, requiring empathy and information design skills. Interviewing subject matter experts to extract tacit knowledge and translating it for diverse audiences requires human communication and trust-building. The tools currently reshaping this role include ChatGPT, Grammarly, and Copilot in Word (Microsoft 365 Copilot) — each accelerating routine work while leaving the most complex, context-dependent decisions firmly in human hands.
narrative_para3: B.Tech, BA (English/Journalism), or M.Tech graduates with excellent writing skills and genuine interest in technology are well suited. The ability to learn new software products quickly, ask the right questions to extract information from engineers, and write with clarity for both technical and non-technical audiences are the core traits. Starting at Rs.3-5 lakh, salaries grow to Rs.5.5-9 lakh at 2-3 years, Rs.9-15 lakh at senior technical writer level, and Rs.15-28 lakh for documentation leads and content strategy roles. Certified Professional Technical Communicator (CPTC) — VERIFY Indian availability — is a recognised qualification. Eligible programme levels: UG, PG.

narrative_para1: A junior Automation Test Engineer at an Indian software company spends their week building and maintaining automated test suites using Selenium, Playwright, or Cypress for web applications and Appium for mobile. Daily tasks include writing page object model frameworks in Python or Java, integrating test execution into Jenkins or GitHub Actions CI/CD pipelines, analysing test failure reports from overnight regression runs, and debugging flaky tests. They implement API test automation using RestAssured or Postman Newman collections, collaborate with developers on testability improvements, and maintain test data management strategies. Indian service companies with large QA practices often expect automation engineers to support performance testing using JMeter or Gatling in addition to functional automation.
narrative_para2: With 65% AI exposure, this role is actively AI-Evolving. GitHub Copilot and ChatGPT generate Selenium and Playwright test scripts, page object models, and test data factories from feature descriptions, dramatically reducing script authoring time. Tabnine completes automation code patterns in real time. AI-powered test generation tools are beginning to auto-generate test cases from user stories and application behaviour. However, automation test engineers design test architectures that are maintainable, scalable, and aligned to CI/CD pipeline needs — requiring systems thinking beyond script generation. They diagnose flaky test root causes involving timing, environment inconsistencies, or upstream system behaviour requiring deep investigation. Deciding which tests to automate vs keep manual, and optimising test suite execution time, requires strategic judgement. The tools currently reshaping this role include GitHub Copilot, ChatGPT, and Tabnine — each accelerating routine work while leaving the most complex, context-dependent decisions firmly in human hands.
narrative_para3: B.Tech, BCA, or MCA graduates with programming aptitude and interest in quality engineering are well suited. Those who enjoy building robust systems — where the 'product' is the test framework itself — and have the patience to debug intermittent failures will thrive. Starting at Rs.3.5-6 lakh, salaries grow to Rs.7-12 lakh at 2-3 years, Rs.12-20 lakh at senior automation engineer level, and Rs.20-35 lakh for test architects and automation leads. ISTQB Advanced (Test Automation Engineer) is a recognised qualification. Strong Selenium and CI/CD skills are the most in-demand differentiators. Eligible programme levels: UG, PG.

narrative_para1: A junior Site Reliability Engineer at an Indian product company spends their week maintaining observability infrastructure using Prometheus, Grafana, and Datadog, writing and updating runbooks for common failure scenarios, and participating in on-call rotations to respond to production incidents. Daily tasks include improving alerting signal-to-noise ratios, writing Kubernetes operators for automated remediation, conducting post-incident reviews, and building reliability automation scripts in Python. They work closely with development teams to define and track SLOs, identify error budgets, and prioritise reliability improvements against new feature work. Indian product companies with large consumer bases — payments, e-commerce, edtech — demand SREs who can maintain 99.9%+ uptime across services handling millions of concurrent users.
narrative_para2: With 50% AI exposure, this role is actively AI-Evolving. GitHub Copilot and ChatGPT assist in writing runbooks, Kubernetes operators, and alerting rule configurations. Amazon Q Developer helps with AWS-specific reliability automation scripts. AI-powered observability tools like Datadog increasingly surface anomaly correlations and predicted failure modes, augmenting on-call engineer workflows. However, sREs make high-stakes incident response decisions under pressure — rolling back deployments, escalating to engineering teams, and communicating status to leadership — requiring judgement that AI cannot provide in real time. They define and negotiate SLOs with product and engineering teams, balancing reliability goals with velocity, requiring relationship and communication skills. Chaos engineering experiments require careful planning and human oversight. The tools currently reshaping this role include GitHub Copilot, ChatGPT, and Amazon Q Developer (formerly AWS CodeWhisperer) — each accelerating routine work while leaving the most complex, context-dependent decisions firmly in human hands.
narrative_para3: B.Tech or MCA graduates with strong Linux, Python, and distributed systems knowledge are the core fit — typically entering after 1-2 years as a DevOps or backend engineer. Those who thrive under pressure, enjoy systematic root cause analysis, and can communicate clearly during high-stakes incidents will excel. Starting at Rs.6-10 lakh, salaries grow to Rs.12-20 lakh at 2-3 years, Rs.20-35 lakh at senior SRE level, and Rs.35-70 lakh (VERIFY) for staff SRE roles. AWS/GCP Professional certifications and Kubernetes expertise (CKA) are strong differentiators. Eligible programme levels: UG, PG.

narrative_para1: A junior Solutions Architect at an Indian IT services firm, cloud consulting company, or enterprise technology team spends their week designing cloud or on-premise solution architectures for client requirements, preparing RFP and RFI responses, and creating architecture diagrams using draw.io or the AWS Architecture Centre templates. Daily tasks include conducting technical discovery sessions with clients, evaluating AWS, Azure, and GCP services for fit against requirements, preparing proof-of-concept implementations in Terraform, and reviewing architecture designs for security, scalability, and cost efficiency. They support pre-sales engagements, prepare solution comparison matrices, and document technical architecture decisions for client sign-off. Indian IT services companies engage solutions architects heavily in BFSI, healthcare, and government digital transformation programmes.
narrative_para2: With 50% AI exposure, this role is actively AI-Evolving. ChatGPT and Amazon Q Developer assist in generating architecture diagrams from text descriptions, drafting RFP responses, and researching AWS and Azure service capabilities. Perplexity AI accelerates research on emerging architecture patterns and cloud service comparisons. AI tools help in generating proof-of-concept IaC and reviewing architecture for common anti-patterns. However, solutions architects translate ambiguous business requirements into concrete technical architectures, requiring deep stakeholder engagement and business domain understanding. They make technology selection decisions with long-term strategic and financial implications — balancing vendor lock-in, team capability, and total cost of ownership. Presenting architectures to executive and technical audiences and defending trade-off decisions requires communication and credibility built through experience. The tools currently reshaping this role include ChatGPT, Amazon Q Developer (formerly AWS CodeWhisperer), and Perplexity AI — each accelerating routine work while leaving the most complex, context-dependent decisions firmly in human hands.
narrative_para3: PG, MBA, or experienced B.Tech graduates (typically with 3-5 years of prior engineering experience) are the usual route into this role. Those with strong technical depth across cloud and infrastructure, combined with the ability to communicate architectural trade-offs clearly to non-technical stakeholders, will thrive. Starting at Rs.7-12 lakh, salaries grow to Rs.14-22 lakh at 2-3 years, Rs.22-35 lakh at solutions architect level, and Rs.35-75 lakh (VERIFY) for principal or distinguished architect roles. AWS Certified Solutions Architect Professional and AWS Well-Architected training are the most valued credentials. Eligible programme levels: PG, MBA.

narrative_para1: A junior Blockchain Developer at an Indian Web3 startup or enterprise blockchain project spends their week writing and testing Solidity smart contracts for Ethereum or Polygon, deploying contracts to testnets using Hardhat or Foundry, and integrating smart contracts with frontend applications via ethers.js or web3.js. Daily tasks include writing unit tests for contract functions, debugging failed transactions using block explorers like Polygonscan, reviewing gas consumption for contract optimisation, and integrating with wallets such as MetaMask. In enterprise contexts, they may work on Hyperledger Fabric or Quorum networks for permissioned blockchain use cases in supply chain or trade finance. Given India's evolving VDA regulatory landscape post the 2022 crypto tax rules, developers also track SEBI and RBI circulars for compliance implications.
narrative_para2: With 50% AI exposure, this role is actively AI-Evolving. GitHub Copilot and ChatGPT assist in writing Solidity contracts, generating ERC-20 and ERC-721 boilerplate, and creating deployment scripts. ChatGPT helps debug transaction failures, analyse gas optimisation, and explain complex cryptographic primitives. AI tools assist in generating test suites for smart contracts using Hardhat and Foundry frameworks. However, blockchain developers conduct smart contract security audits to identify reentrancy, overflow, and access control vulnerabilities — high-stakes work where AI-generated code frequently introduces exploitable bugs. They design token economics and governance models balancing incentives across diverse stakeholder groups, requiring economic reasoning and game theory knowledge. Navigating India's evolving VDA (Virtual Digital Asset) regulatory framework requires up-to-date legal and compliance awareness. The tools currently reshaping this role include GitHub Copilot, ChatGPT, and Tabnine — each accelerating routine work while leaving the most complex, context-dependent decisions firmly in human hands.
narrative_para3: B.Tech or MCA graduates with strong JavaScript or Python fundamentals and curiosity about cryptographic systems are well positioned. Comfort with command-line tools, testing discipline for security-critical smart contract code, and awareness of blockchain economics are important traits. Starting at Rs.5-9 lakh, salaries grow to Rs.10-18 lakh at 2-3 years, Rs.18-30 lakh at senior developer level, and Rs.30-65 lakh (VERIFY) for blockchain architects and protocol engineers. Certified Blockchain Developer (CBDE — VERIFY) and Ethereum developer certifications are available. A strong portfolio of audited, deployed smart contracts is the most effective hiring signal. Eligible programme levels: UG, PG.

narrative_para1: A junior Game Developer at an Indian mobile or PC game studio spends their week writing gameplay scripts in C# (Unity) or C++ (Unreal Engine), implementing game mechanics from designer specifications, and fixing bugs found during internal playtesting. Daily tasks include building game systems such as inventory, save state, and enemy AI, integrating mobile SDKs for analytics (Firebase) and in-app purchases (Google Play Billing), and profiling game performance on target Android and iOS devices. Indian mobile game studios primarily target the hyper-casual and mid-core mobile segments — requiring developers to optimise aggressively for lower-spec Android devices common in India's mass market, and to integrate monetisation systems including rewarded ads and battle passes.
narrative_para2: With 45% AI exposure, this role is actively AI-Evolving. GitHub Copilot assists in generating game mechanic scripts, AI NPC behaviour trees, and boilerplate systems like inventory management in C# and C++. ChatGPT supports game loop architecture decisions, shader debugging, and writing tool scripts. Midjourney and generative AI tools are used for rapid concept art, texture generation, and asset ideation in indie and mid-size studios. However, game developers design the core gameplay loop, pacing, and feel — elements requiring creative vision and iterative player testing that AI cannot replicate. They optimise rendering and CPU performance for target hardware, applying platform-specific profiling knowledge that demands deep technical expertise. Collaboration with artists, sound designers, and game designers to achieve a cohesive player experience requires human creative direction. The tools currently reshaping this role include GitHub Copilot, ChatGPT, and Midjourney — each accelerating routine work while leaving the most complex, context-dependent decisions firmly in human hands.
narrative_para3: B.Tech, BCA, or BDes graduates with a passion for games and a portfolio of completed game projects are well positioned. Those who combine programming discipline with creative instincts, and enjoy building systems that create engaging player experiences, will thrive. Starting at Rs.3.5-6 lakh, salaries grow to Rs.7-12 lakh at 2-3 years, Rs.12-20 lakh at senior developer level, and Rs.20-40 lakh (VERIFY) for technical directors and game architecture leads. Unity Certified Professional (VERIFY) is available. A portfolio of shipped or playable games is the strongest hiring signal. Eligible programme levels: UG, PG.

narrative_para1: A junior Embedded Systems Engineer at an Indian hardware startup, automotive electronics firm, or industrial IoT company spends their week writing firmware in C and C++ for microcontrollers such as STM32 or ESP32, debugging hardware-software integration issues using oscilloscopes and JTAG debuggers, and reviewing hardware schematics to understand peripheral interfacing requirements. Daily tasks include implementing communication protocols such as UART, SPI, I2C, and CAN, writing interrupt service routines, optimising firmware for real-time performance on RTOS platforms like FreeRTOS, and collaborating with hardware engineers on board bring-up for new PCB revisions. Indian automotive electronics suppliers and industrial automation companies are active employers in this space, particularly in Pune, Bengaluru, and Chennai.
narrative_para2: With 35% AI exposure, this role is actively AI-Evolving. GitHub Copilot and ChatGPT assist in generating peripheral driver templates, HAL abstraction layer code, and unit test stubs for embedded C/C++ codebases. ChatGPT supports debugging register-level issues and interpreting microcontroller datasheets. AI tools are beginning to assist with hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) test script generation. However, embedded systems engineers debug hardware-software interaction issues using oscilloscopes, logic analysers, and JTAG debuggers — skills requiring physical hardware access and deep electronics knowledge AI cannot replicate. They optimise firmware for power consumption, memory footprint, and real-time determinism, requiring trade-off decisions specific to each hardware platform. Hardware bring-up on new PCB revisions requires hands-on investigation skills. The tools currently reshaping this role include GitHub Copilot, ChatGPT, and Tabnine — each accelerating routine work while leaving the most complex, context-dependent decisions firmly in human hands.
narrative_para3: B.Tech in Electronics, ECE, or EEE graduates are the core fit. Those who enjoy working at the boundary of software and hardware, are comfortable with a soldering iron and an oscilloscope, and have patience for low-level debugging will thrive. This is predominantly an on-site role due to hardware lab dependency. Starting at Rs.3.5-6 lakh, salaries grow to Rs.7-12 lakh at 2-3 years, Rs.12-20 lakh at senior engineer level, and Rs.20-40 lakh for principal firmware engineers. ARM Cortex-M certifications and AUTOSAR familiarity are valuable for automotive segment careers. Eligible programme levels: UG, PG.

narrative_para1: A junior IoT Developer at an Indian smart device startup, industrial automation company, or agritech firm spends their week writing firmware for ESP32 or Raspberry Pi-based devices, integrating sensors (temperature, humidity, GPS, accelerometer) with cloud platforms like AWS IoT Core or Azure IoT Hub, and building backend services to process device telemetry data. Daily tasks include configuring MQTT message brokers, writing Python scripts for edge data preprocessing using AWS Greengrass, debugging device connectivity issues in field environments, and maintaining device management dashboards. They coordinate with hardware engineers on PCB design reviews and work with mobile developers on companion app integrations. Indian smart agriculture, smart manufacturing, and smart city projects create active demand for IoT developers comfortable across both firmware and cloud layers.
narrative_para2: With 40% AI exposure, this role is actively AI-Evolving. GitHub Copilot and ChatGPT generate firmware code, cloud integration scripts, and AWS IoT Core rule configurations. Amazon Q Developer assists with AWS IoT device management and Greengrass component development. AI tools help interpret sensor datasheet protocols and generate device shadow handling code. However, ioT developers physically commission and debug device fleets in field environments — warehouses, factories, agricultural fields — where remote debugging is insufficient and hands-on troubleshooting is essential. They design power-efficient firmware for battery-operated devices that must operate for years without maintenance, requiring hardware-specific optimisation. Security architecture decisions for devices that cannot be easily patched post-deployment require careful upfront human design. The tools currently reshaping this role include GitHub Copilot, ChatGPT, and Amazon Q Developer (formerly AWS CodeWhisperer) — each accelerating routine work while leaving the most complex, context-dependent decisions firmly in human hands.
narrative_para3: B.Tech in CS, ECE, or EEE graduates with interest in both hardware and cloud development are well positioned. Those who enjoy solving problems across the full IoT stack — from sensor interfacing to cloud dashboards — and are comfortable with field deployment challenges will thrive. Starting at Rs.3.5-6 lakh, salaries grow to Rs.7-12 lakh at 2-3 years, Rs.12-20 lakh at senior developer level, and Rs.20-40 lakh for IoT architects. AWS IoT certification and hands-on hardware project portfolios are strong differentiators. Eligible programme levels: UG, PG.

narrative_para1: A junior IT Support Engineer at an Indian enterprise, BPO, or IT services company spends their week resolving end-user tickets in ServiceNow covering issues such as laptop hardware failures, VPN connectivity problems, software installation requests, and Microsoft 365 access issues. Daily tasks include provisioning and deprovisioning user accounts in Azure Active Directory (Entra ID), configuring endpoints with Microsoft Intune, supporting video conferencing setups, and maintaining hardware inventory records. They handle printer and network peripheral troubleshooting, escalate complex issues to L2 or L3 teams, and contribute to the internal knowledge base by documenting recurring issue resolutions. Indian BPO and IT services environments operate 24x7 support models requiring shift flexibility, while enterprise IT teams focus on business-hours support with on-call coverage.
narrative_para2: With 45% AI exposure, this role is actively AI-Evolving. ChatGPT and Microsoft Copilot assist IT support engineers in generating step-by-step troubleshooting guides, drafting knowledge base articles, and researching solutions for uncommon error codes. AI-powered service desk tools increasingly auto-suggest resolutions from historical tickets, reducing resolution time. Notion AI helps maintain and update internal runbooks and SOPs. However, iT support engineers provide empathetic, context-sensitive assistance to non-technical users who need patient, clear guidance — human communication skills AI chatbots cannot fully replicate. They diagnose complex environmental issues involving hardware failures, software conflicts, and network intermittency that require physical investigation. Escalation decisions, vendor management during hardware failures, and executive-level support require human judgement and accountability. The tools currently reshaping this role include ChatGPT, Microsoft Copilot, and Notion AI — each accelerating routine work while leaving the most complex, context-dependent decisions firmly in human hands.
narrative_para3: Any graduate or BCA/B.Tech candidate with strong communication skills and a patient, service-oriented mindset is well suited. Those who genuinely enjoy helping people with technology problems, can explain technical concepts clearly to non-technical users, and stay calm under complaint pressure will thrive. Starting at Rs.2.5-4 lakh, salaries grow to Rs.4.5-7 lakh at 2-3 years, Rs.7-12 lakh at L2/L3 support or team lead level, and Rs.12-22 lakh for IT manager roles. CompTIA A+, ITIL Foundation certification, and Microsoft 365 certifications are the most valued credentials. This role offers a strong entry point into broader IT infrastructure and cloud engineering careers. Eligible programme levels: UG, PG.
